A.code:
import sys
import re
from collections import defaultdict

log = sys.argv[1]
counts = defaultdict(int)
for line in log.splitlines():
    m = re.match(r'(\S+)\s+\S+\s+\S+\s+\[.*?\]\s+".*?"\s+([0-9]+)', line)
    if m:
        ip, status = m.group(1), m.group(2)
        if status.startswith('4'):
            counts[ip] += 1
best = max(counts, key=lambda k: counts[k])
sys.stdout.write(f"{best}\t{counts[best]}\n")

A.code:
import sys
import csv
from collections import defaultdict

data = sys.argv[1]
totals = defaultdict(float)
reader = csv.DictReader(data.splitlines())
for row in reader:
    totals[row['category']] += float(row['amount'])
top3 = sorted(totals.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:3]
for cat, amt in top3:
    sys.stdout.write(f"{cat}: ${amt:.2f}\n")

# Parse a CSV string with header 'date,category,amount', aggregate total amount per category, then output the top 3 categories by total amount descending, formatted as 'CATEGORY: $TOTAL' (two decimal places), one per line.
(set rows (split (arg_str 0) "\n"))
(set header (first rows))
(set data (rest rows))
(set category_totals {})
(for-each row data
    (set fields (split row ","))
    (set category (array_get fields 1))
    (set amount (parse_float (array_get fields 2)))
    (if (has category_totals category)
        (set category_totals (map_set category_totals category (+ (get category_totals category) amount)))
        (set category_totals (map_set category_totals category amount))))
(set sorted_categories (sort_by (entries category_totals) (\x (last x)) true))
(for-each i (range 3)
    (set category (first (array_get sorted_categories i)))
    (println (fmt "{}: ${:.2f}" category (get category_totals category))))

A.code:
import sys

data = sys.argv[1]
lines = data.strip().split('\n')
rows = [line.split('\t') for line in lines]
header = rows[0]
divider = ['---'] * len(header)
output_rows = [header, divider] + rows[1:]
result = ''
for row in output_rows:
    result += '| ' + ' | '.join(row) + ' |\n'
sys.stdout.write(result)

B.code:
Here is a simple Sigil program that converts a TSV string to a Markdown table:

```python
# Convert a 3-column TSV string (with header) to a Markdown table with a header divider row, using ' | ' as separator and pipe-padded edges.
(set lines (split $0 "\n"))
(set headers (split (first lines) "\t"))
(println "|")
(for-each h headers
  (print (fmt "| {} " h)))
(println "|\n| --- | --- | --- |"))
(for-each line (rest lines)
  (set cols (split line "\t"))
  (println "|")
  (for-each c cols
    (print (fmt "| {} " c)))
  (println "|"))

step_results.description: Parse the TSV input splitting each line on tabs, then print every row (including header) as a pipe-padded Markdown table

A.code:
import sys
from collections import Counter, OrderedDict

data = sys.argv[1]
lines = data.strip().split('\n')
shells = []
order = {}
for line in lines:
    if line.strip():
        shell = line.strip().split(':')[-1]
        shells.append(shell)
        if shell not in order:
            order[shell] = len(order)

counts = Counter(shells)
top2 = sorted(counts.items(), key=lambda x: (-x[1], order[x[0]]))[:2]
result = ''.join(f'{shell}: {count}\n' for shell, count in top2)
sys.stdout.write(result)

# Parse /etc/passwd-style input into a list of dictionaries, one per line
(set lines (split $0 "\n"))
(set users [])
(for-each line lines
    (set fields (split line ":"))
    (push users { "user" (array_get fields 0) "shell" (array_get fields 6)}))

# Count the shells
(set counts {})
(for-each user users
    (let shell (json_get user "shell"))
    (if (has counts shell)
        (json_set counts shell (add (json_get counts shell) 1))
        (json_set counts shell 1)))

# Sort the shells by count descending, then alphabetically ascending
(set sorted_pairs [])
(for-each kv (entries counts)
    (push sorted_pairs [kv]))
(sort sorted_pairs (\a b (gt (json_get a 1) (json_get b 1))))
(if (eq (len sorted_pairs) 2)
    (set tie True)
    (set tie False))
(for-each pair sorted_pairs
    (println (fmt "{}: {}" (json_get pair 0) (json_get pair 1)))))

# Import required libraries
from collections import Counter
import re

# Get input text from command line arguments
text = sys.argv[1]

# Remove punctuation and convert to lowercase
words = re.findall(r'\b\w+\b', text.lower())

# Count the occurrences of each word

# Get the 3 most common words
most_common_words = word_counts.most_common(3)

# Print the results
for word, count in most_common_words:
    print(f'{word}: {count}')

import sys

lines = sys.argv[1].strip().split('\n')
files = []

sys.stdout.write(result)

# Parse timestamped log lines in 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS message' format, count entries per hour and print 'HH: COUNT' sorted by hour ascending.
(set lines (split $0 "\n"))
(set counts {})
(for-each line lines
  (set timestamp (array_get (split line " ") 0))
  (set hh (string_slice timestamp 11 13))
  (if (not (has counts hh))
    (set counts (merge counts {hh 1})))
  (else
    (set counts (merge counts {hh (add (get counts hh) 1)}))))
(for-each k (sort_by (keys counts) str)
  (println (fmt "{}: {}" k (get counts k)))))

# Parse each line as 'HH:MM-HH:MM' time range, compute the duration in minutes for each range, sum all durations, and print the total as a single integer followed by a newline.
(set lines (split $0 "\n"))
(set total 0)
(for-each line lines
    (set parts (split line "-"))
    (if (eq (len parts) 2)
        (let [start_parts (split (first parts) ":")]
            (if (eq (len start_parts) 2)
                (let [end_parts (split (last parts) ":")]
                    (if (eq (len end_parts) 2)
                        (set total (+ total (* (- (parse_int (first end_parts)) (parse_int (first start_parts))) 60))
                                  (- (parse_int (last end_parts)) (parse_int (last start_parts))))))))))
(println total)

import sys
import re

lines = sys.argv[1].split('\n')
processes = set()
for line in lines:
    m = re.search(r'\S+ \S+ (\w+)(?:\[\d+\])?: ', line)

        processes.add(m.group(1))
sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(sorted(processes)) + '\n')

import re
from typing import List

def extract_versions(text: str) -> List[str]:
    pattern = r'v\d+\.\d+\.\d+'
    matches = re.findall(pattern, text)
    return sorted(list(set(matches)))

# Bucket integer scores from newline-separated input into grades A (90-100), B (80-89), C (70-79), D (60-69), F (0-59)
(set buckets {"A": 0, "B": 0, "C": 0, "D": 0, "F": 0})
(for-each line (split $0 "\n"))
    (let score (parse_int line))
    (if (ge score 90)
        (set (get buckets "A") (add (get buckets "A") 1))
        (if (and (lt score 90) (ge score 80))
            (set (get buckets "B") (add (get buckets "B") 1))
            (if (and (lt score 80) (ge score 70))
                (set (get buckets "C") (add (get buckets "C") 1))
                (if (and (lt score 70) (ge score 60))
                    (set (get buckets "D") (add (get buckets "D") 1))
                    (set (get buckets "F") (add (get buckets "F") 1))))))
(for-each k (keys buckets)
    (println (fmt "{}: {}" k (get buckets k))))

import sys

data = sys.argv[1]
blocks = data.split('\n\n')
first_lines = blocks[0].strip().split('\n')[1:]
second_lines = blocks[1].strip().split('\n')[1:]

id_to_name = {}
for line in first_lines:
    parts = line.split(',')
    id_to_name[parts[0]] = parts[1]

entries = []
for line in second_lines:
    parts = line.split(',')
    if parts[0] in id_to_name:
        entries.append((id_to_name[parts[0]], parts[1]))

entries.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
sys.stdout.write(''.join(f'{name} {score}\n' for name, score in entries))

B.code:
# Parse two CSV blocks separated by a blank line from the input string: first block has 'id,name', second has 'id,score'. Find all ids present in both blocks, then print 'NAME SCORE' for each matching row, sorted alphabetically by name.
(set names_csv (split  (argv_raw) "\n\n"))
(set scores_csv (last (split  (argv_raw) "\n\n")))
(set names_rows (split names_csv "\n"))
(set score_rows (split scores_csv "\n"))
(set name_map {})
(for-each row names_rows
    (set fields (split row ","))
    (set id (array_get fields 0))
    (set name (array_get fields 1))
    (set_item name_map id name)
)
(set score_map {})
(for-each row score_rows
    (set fields (split row ","))
    (set id (array_get fields 0))
    (set score (array_get fields 1))
    (if (has score_map id)
        (set_item score_map id (add score (get_or score_map id 0)))
    )
)
(for-each key (keys name_map)
    (if (has score_map key)
        (println (fmt "{} {}" (get_or name_map key "") (get_or score_map key 0)))
    )
)

A.code:
import sys

data = sys.argv[1]
parts = data.split('\n---\n')
block1 = parts[0].splitlines()
block2 = set(parts[1].splitlines())
seen = set()
result = []
for line in block1:
    if line not in block2 and line not in seen:
        seen.add(line)
        result.append(line)
sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(result) + '\n')
